justification: All 7 business rules identified with correct invariant classifications. Refund window correctly marked invariant: false with sound reasoning. Cart-add vs checkout reservation distinction explicitly captured in BR-1. Discount code / promotion mutual exclusivity captured in BR-4. Multiple ambiguities surfaced (discount precedence, refund vs immutability, price-locking semantics, 15-minute timer start point). 11 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided, well exceeding the 4 minimum. Confidence level of 72 is appropriate given the stated assumptions and unresolved ambiguities. Notable: BR-2 (reservation timeout) was marked invariant: false with nuanced reasoning that the duration is policy but the expiry principle is invariant — a defensible and sophisticated distinction.

observations.text: All 7 required business rules identified and correctly classified
Refund window (BR-7) correctly marked invariant: false with clear policy reasoning
Cart-does-not-reserve-inventory rule (BR-1) explicitly and prominently stated
Discount code / promotion mutual exclusivity (BR-4) clearly captured
11 acceptance criteria — nearly 3x the minimum requirement, with good coverage of boundary conditions (AC-10, AC-11)
Rich ambiguity escalations covering discount precedence, refund modeling, and price-locking semantics
Ubiquitous language section is thorough and domain-accurate
Edge cases section identifies race conditions, mid-checkout promotion changes, and partial refund gaps
BR-2 reservation timeout marked invariant: false with nuanced reasoning — distinguishes the policy threshold from the invariant principle of expiry
The specific ambiguity about what constitutes 'delivery' for the refund window (courier scan vs customer confirmation) was not explicitly flagged as an escalation
Confidence level appropriately moderate at 72 with well-articulated high/low confidence areas and explicit assumptions
